title: Classification: Naive Bayes for Text and Tabular Data
content:
### Day 102: Classification: Naive Bayes for Text and Tabular Data

**Probability:** P(A) = favorable outcomes / total outcomes

**Bayes Theorem:** P(A|B) = P(B|A) × P(A) / P(B)
- Update beliefs with new evidence
- Foundation of Naive Bayes classifier
codeExample:
# Day 102: Classification: Naive Bayes for Text and Tabular Data
# Probability basics
print("=== Probability ===")
print(f"Coin flip: P(heads) = {1/2}")
print(f"Die roll P(even): {3/6}")

# Bayes Theorem
print("\n=== Bayes: Disease Testing ===")
P_disease = 0.001      # 0.1% have disease
P_pos_given_disease = 0.99  # 99% sensitivity
P_pos_given_healthy = 0.05  # 5% false positive
P_healthy = 1 - P_disease

P_pos = P_pos_given_disease * P_disease + P_pos_given_healthy * P_healthy
P_disease_given_pos = (P_pos_given_disease * P_disease) / P_pos

print(f"Test positive → actually sick: {P_disease_given_pos:.1%}")
print("Even with 99% accurate test, only ~2% chance if disease is rare!")
practiceQuestions: How is Bayes used in spam filtering?
